Checking 8am
27 pieces
Checking 10am
Checking 11am
Checking 12
Checking 1pm

An accumulation of bags (76) was observed in the X-ray machine's reject bin after I returned from my lunch break. Production was halted for 13 minutes so that Quality could inspect the situation. All rejected bags were checked by passing them through the X-ray machine twice, turning them over during the second pass to ensure a thorough inspection. No anomalies were found, and the product was released. Charity Young Victor Romero Sr.

MM6 and MM7 print (BB and lot#) needs an improvement, lead and supervisor informed and they are working in improvements right now


Charity Young
Thank you
Shift 3
Everything is on night mode or off

QA completed the inspection and ATP pass(33/20)
Charity Young
Any update on these?
They are just arriving in L1. When they are unloaded they will be inspected by QA
At the moment, the QA is inspecting.
Weight checks - 11:00am.
Weight checks - 12:00pm.

Are we going to do a mix of totes?

Justin Garner. How close will this get us to the order?

The pallets have been released to be mixed 
Charity Young, Justin Garner, Victor Romero Sr.
The 5 totes will be released. Mariela will let you know when it’s complete.
QA. has released the 10112 pallets and will start mixing the product. Curt Willis Justin Garner
We will still be around 65-70 pallets short.
We are now mixing the small and big macadamia pallets, one bucket of each as per Q.C.

A piece of paper was found in the partial pallet left by 3rd shift while the operator was preparing it for use. The pallet will be placed on Hold 
Charity Young Victor Romero Sr., Justin Garner
Thank you.
Justin Garner do you want to sift through that pallet?
Weight checks - EoS.
An employee will check the product before using it. Charity Young
Enrober SoS
Decorator SoS
We've paused the line due to running out of caramel. We will resume as soon as the next batch is ready, should be less than 5 minutes. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Line is running again.
The cooling table for Tunnel 1 was on. We turned it off to follow centerline.
The enrober blower was on at the beginning of the shift and was turned off.
We were unable to find the S3 team lead radio today
Dakota Adair Victor Romero Sr. Nathan Topham

The team just noticed that the Tunnel 1 belt isn't passing under this roller as it's supposed to, meaning that the belt is dragging against the framework underneath the shaker. We've stopped depositing so that we can release the tension of the Tunnel 1 belt and put the roller/belt in the correct position. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
The team fixed it already. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Paused for caramel.  There is a problem with the pump
Maintenance is looking at it

The pump doesn't seem to be creating much suction... one tote was nearly empty, and the other was about half-full, but either one should have worked for filling the mixing tank. To be certain it's not an issue with either tote, we changed the nearly-empty tote of condensed milk for a brand new tote. We're waiting to see if that pumps any better while Jon Jensen continues to investigate the issue. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Changing the tote for a new one had no effect.

Maintenance tried opening a drain valve after the pump, and it sprayed mostly air at first. After a minute, it began pouring the condensed milk, and we then saw the condensed milk reaching the mixing tank. We're starting to cook caramel again now, and the kitchen operators estimate we'll be ready to resume depositing in about 35-40 minutes. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
We've resumed depositing now. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Depositor EoS
The caramel depositor is leaking
We are pausing the line due to the leaking Depositor
Enrober EoS

Weight= 940


Pieces= 45

We replaced some seals on the caramel depositor, while doing the cleaning of the plate we lose the temperature on the hopper
 
We will run untill 4 in the front 4:30 in the back so we can complete the 4h run and then we will clean level 1 from 4:30 to 7:30 so it can be a list 3h of cleaning 
 
Curt Willis
The hose for caramel depositor bulged, we replaced with the one maintenance found we cleaned and ATP passed

We end with 2 1/2 totes of mac 20112 Costco and 4 of the 20112, and 468 tray 
 
The tray amount is about 2h-2h1/2 of production for monday 
Curt Willis,Justin Garner

At the start of the shift we had 70 pallets remaining we did 15


 


Still 55 remaining

The 10 pallets of rework are here they are in warehouse area 





Curt Willis,Jody Howse 


We weren't unable to work on this, we will have to do it on Monday
Everything is off or in night mode
Countdown
Maintenance is replacing / added two shim. That were dull on the Cavanna
Shim replaced running at 16mpm.

Thanks Rich Brierley

Frederick Peairs

When Scott from Cavanna gets there could you let me know?
No open wrap is making it to FG.

Rose Smuin Chad Stone Rich Brierley Preston Elmer Shawn Jewkes Bosch is able to keep up with the extra bars i am making adjustments to the the arms above the belt to help get bars straightened before they hit Bosch
Chad Stone Scott is here.
We tried to send 5 peices to the bosch at 16mpm and it was too much for the operator to keep up. Currently we are at 4 peices running 16mpm
Chad Stone Rich Brierley Preston Elmer Shawn Jewkes the adjustments I made are help get each bar single file into the Bosch

Rose Smuin Chad Stone Rich Brierley Preston Elmer Shawn Jewkes Bosch is able to keep up with the extra bars i am making adjustments to the the arms above the belt to help get bars straightened before they hit Bosch
Looks great, man! We'll have to get measurements and mark some centerline positions so we can reproduce this!

Chad Stone Rose Smuin Rich Brierley Rich Brierley Shawn Jewkes Nathan Gevenois We began sending 4 pieces of chocolate to the Bosch instead of 3 and have experienced no issues. Once Maintenance completes the necessary adjustments to the Cavanna, we will test running at 16 MPM.
I am currently making adjustments for all chocolate feeding to cavanna trying to get it single file best we can to improve flow though the cavanna
Chad Stone Rose Smuin Rich Brierley Preston Elmer After I make these adjustments do you see any reason we can't try 17 mpm ?
We have increased speed and efficiency to bosch if I can create efficiency to cavanna i would like to try 17mpm

Ok we have been running constantly at 16 mpm. I have made a lot of adjustments on the chocolate straightening guides. We weren't even using 3 of them. Chocolate is flowing into both machines way better.
Nice work

Weight in the target


Appearance acceptable
Instead of air blowing of peices every 20-50 bars we are now blowing 1 peice off every few hundred bars

I am good with trying especially with Scott there. 

Standard is 15 so if we can run 16 consistently it is a win for us. 🙌

Just manage scrap and efficiency.

Packaging 

104 open product 10 % were found in a box, 2 people checking

87open product  8.3 %  were found in box n•2 , 2 people checking

The main issue of why the front parts of the bar are getting cut randomly is the buildup on the fingers which is adding 2-3mm thus flinging the chocolate forward when it rolls into the down position. We should try cleaning the fingers during lunch break and put that theory to the test.

Chad Stone Rose Smuin Rich Brierley Preston Elmer Shawn Jewkes we have been running at 17mpm for the last 20 minutes bosch and Cavanna are keeping up QA is going to do two box check for open products and give us a percentage
148 open product 14%  were  found in box
When you say box check are you saying what we pulled off the line or what was in a WIP box being sent to L2 Frederick Peairs?
After cleaning the fingers and decreasing speed to 620 from 670 the open product has been reduced

Qa is currently pulling a box and inspecting every piece to verify the number of open bars and calculate the percentage of defects per case. We wanted to evaluate performance at 17 MPM to determine whether the rate of open bars increased significantly.

I just want to make sure we are aligned. 

With us inspecting there should be 0 bars getting into WIP boxes. 

If we can’t do that we definitely need to slow to 16 MPM max.

Chad Stone Itzel Chavez  We have lowered the speed back to 15 MPM to accommodate employee lunch rotations. Prior to doing so, we successfully ran at 17 MPM for one hour.
 
Update:
 
Adjustments were made to all chocolate straightening guides.
 
We increased the feed rate from 3 bars to 4 bars being sent to the Bosch.
 
Line speed was increased from 15 MPM to 17 MPM, and both the Bosch and Cavanna were able to maintain the increased speed without issue. Scott is still working on open bars issue 
 
Scott identified approximately 3 mm of buildup on the Cavanna teeth, which is believed to be the cause of the open bars.
 
The Cavanna side was shut down to clean the teeth, and we are currently testing for open bars at 15 MPM while employees rotate through lunch breaks
We have reduced the speed to 15. 16-17 mpm we need 1-2 more EE's to help inspect the product. Hopefully Scott can fix issue. Scott is seeing a lot of build up in many places.

What is the temperature near the cavannas?

I feel like it may be warm and the chocolate i melting.
Is melting

Its definitely warm in this area

















66.7 in that area of build up
Bars are jumping around due to the build up

It was observed we are cleaning we are using dull scrapers knocking high spots off but still leaving behind residue. Its not solving the issue. Scott suggested doing a full clean on heavy build up areas then wipe with a sanitation wipe the clean surfaces the chocolate rides on

Chad Stone the cleaner we get the chocolate guides on the Cavanna the less open bars that are produced this is what Scott is observing. He said we should be able to run at 17 mpm with no issue once we fix the cleaning issue on cavanna.
Line is running at 16mpm now.
We keep stopping every 10min to clean cavanna.
Everyone of these readings was of the plexiglass. iR temp guns don't see through anything.
Scott from Cavanna just left.
The belt c4 and C6 running good

An inspection was carried out on two final boxes in box 1 there were 1051 pieces of which 21 were open which makes 2%, in box 2 there were 1054 pieces of which there were 25 open equal to 2.3%
 The number of open pieces is now much smaller. Thank Itzel Chavez Frederick Peairs
We paused at the end of the start of shift to do a more thorough CIL we are starting back up and will got 30min. Until the next one
We went about 20 minutes before we started having problems
Glycol is good
Start up checklist
We are down for an inverter fault on Chicane 2

We have started back up we had to do a reset on the Cavanna. 
While we were down we noticed this roller had gone bad on one of the infeed belts and there was a lot of build up of chocolate maintenance had a new one so we replaced it. 


The belt will need to be trimmed tonight
Mid Shift
Metal detector belt, chain and belt of imperial, Demolding and c6 belt are running correctly
MD belt and C6 tracking correctly
We’re still having lots of issues with bars, not kicking off correctly and then jamming when they go to get wrapped.
Weight of the bars are within target range. Bars are smooth and in acceptable condition. Lot is legible and present on both Cavannas
Glycol is good
Metal detector belt, chain and belt of imperial, Demolding and c6 belt are running correctly
Start-of-shift verification (3rd shift): part weight, organoleptic properties, packaging, labeling, tempering, and Sweco—all within the correct range.
Lot change CC26165I1, date 12/Feb/28, correct.
C1 belt has briefly stopped 3 times
Run was complete at 2am, team went to lunch and level 2 clean has started.
ATP plate and ATP sweco screen correct
ATP molds correct
ATP demolding correct
ATP filter PTL correct
Belt C2 is having edges cut by QA request
Chad we moved the depositor storage where you wanted it. If we have time after lunches we will gather up the parts and start putting them together on the storage rack
Molds passed ATP👍
Scraper bin valve OPL attached near pump
Preston Kevin caramel samples were left by depositor two I’m not sure if you guys still needed these?
I already drop some samples   to R&D but could you leave it by the QA desk? I’ll verify if I need to drop them on Monday. Thanks!!
ATP molds correct

Daniel Bringas Amy Hadrich

I’ve asked Brandon Hernandez to run a hour longer than normal tonight if needed to use up all KKL nuts before stopping.  

If that happens Justin Garner and Katya Farnsworth you will need to extra time on Monday morning to complete the cleaning before starting up PRS Mac nuts.  

Brandon please call out how long you cleaned on S3 so we can be prepared and spend extra time on Monday if needed

Curt Willis, we received the last pallet of trays from SV around 9:00pm, meaning that we'll run out of trays before we run out of macadamia nuts. We currently have 6 full totes of macadamia nuts and two partial totes, which would last around 7 hours, but we only have enough trays to last maybe 4 hours.
Level 2 clean completed and passed off ATP
Cute Cavanna is now reassembled as requested by 3rd shift and good to go
Is that on chicane belt 3?
Yes
I noticed this when walking by today. Not sure if it has been brought up but we will need to trim the belt.
Posted OPL for Operators to check alarms on Chicane HMi
I'll get it handled, thanks!
Level 2 clean completed and passed off ATP
Can we send all the WIP caramel to L3? Justin Garner Curt Willis Charity Young
